title: T-074 — RM usability validation shared decision service
prompt:
# T-074 — RM usability validation shared decision service

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §0, §5.1A, §7.1, §7.6
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Shared RM usability decision service and server-side enforcement contract.

This task must centralize semantics; do not duplicate ad-hoc checks in BOM actions. Supplier approval may be read from supplier_specs.supplier_status in Phase 1 or supplier master if available. Lab/QC status is read-only from Quality-owned read model; absence of required QC status must be a typed warning/block according to context. No UI implementation in this task. Enforcement consumers must call this service at BOM line add/edit, bundle approval, PO receipt review, and Production material issue seams; failing hard-block checks must be machine-readable for UI panels and APIs.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- apps/web/lib/technical/rm-usability.ts [create]
- apps/web/lib/technical/rm-usability.test.ts [create]
- apps/web/actions/technical/boms/validate-component.ts [create]
- tests/actions/technical/rm-usability.integration.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given item status is blocked, when validateRmUsability runs, then usable=false and reason code ITEM_NOT_ACTIVE is returned.
2. Given supplier_spec is expired or missing, then reason code SUPPLIER_SPEC_NOT_ACTIVE is returned.
3. Given RM allergens conflict with target FG allergen constraints, then reason code ALLERGEN_CONFLICT is returned with allergen codes.
4. Given all checks pass, then usable=true and all check rows are green with source references.
5. Given context=bom_edit and validateRmUsability returns a blocking reason, then BOM line add/edit service rejects the change with the same code and no line mutation.
6. Given context=factory_spec_approval and Quality read model says required QC release is missing, then approval is blocked or warned according to configured rule and the response includes QC_RELEASE_MISSING.
7. Given UI consumers request the report shape, then check rows include label, code, severity, source, remediation_href and evidence timestamp.

## Test strategy
- Create unit RED table tests for every reason code.
- Run: pnpm vitest run apps/web/lib/technical/rm-usability.test.ts tests/actions/technical/rm-usability.integration.test.ts

## Risk red lines
- Do not own lab workflow in Technical; consume read model only.
- Do not silently approve unknown supplier/spec data.
- Do not return unstructured strings only; callers need typed codes.
- Do not let UI-only warnings replace server-side enforcement for hard blocks.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
